description: In a living room, Ross, Rachel, Monica, and Phoebe sit on sofas. Ross says, "You."
Chandler walks through the front door and stops behind the sofa.
Chandler looks around and asks, "Okay, what is it about me?"
Gesturing with his hands, Chandler asks, "Do I not look fun enough? Is there something repellent about me?"
Ross turns his head toward Chandler.
Ross asks, "So how was the party?" Chandler replies, "Well, it couldn't have been worse. A woman literally passed through me."
Chandler gestures to himself with both hands.
Chandler says, "Okay, so what is it?"
Spreading his arms wide, Chandler asks, "Am I hideously unattractive?"
Phoebe says, "No, you are not. You are very attractive. You know what? I go through the exact same thing. Every time I put on a little weight, I start"
Phoebe continues, "questioning everything," moving her hands as she speaks.
Chandler drops his arms and stares at Phoebe.
Chandler gestures to himself and says, "Whoa, whoa. I put on a little weight?"
Chandler looks down at his waist. Joey enters the apartment and walks into the kitchen behind them.
Phoebe waves a hand and says, "No, not know. More like insulation."
Monica stands up from the sofa and faces Chandler.
Monica gestures to him and says, "Chandler, I'm unemployed, in dire need of a project. You want to work out? I can remake you." Chandler replies, "I would, but that might get"
Chandler continues, "in the way of my lying around."
Ross, Joey, and Rachel speak indistinctly, gesturing toward Chandler.
Chandler says, "Okay. All right. But if we put on spandex and my boobs are bigger than yours, I'm going home." He points a finger at Monica.
Phoebe stands, gesturing toward Chandler as she says, "Your boobs are fine. Look, I never should have said anything. Come here. Come here." She opens her arms.
Phoebe wraps her arms around Chandler, hugging him tightly.
While hugging him, Phoebe pats his back and says, "Oh, can't make hands meet"
Phoebe steps back. Chandler stares at her with his mouth slightly open. The screen turns completely black.
The screen remains black in silence.
An exterior view shows city skyscrapers. A song with vocals begins, singing, "you."
The exterior view of the skyline remains visible as an upbeat instrumental song continues.
The setting changes to an apartment hallway. Monica walks to a door and knocks loudly.
Chandler opens the door wearing a sweatshirt and spandex shorts, immediately jogging in place.
Monica says, "Oh, nothing. Just never seen your little stretchy pants before," as Chandler continues jogging.
Chandler stops jogging, pausing to look down at his shorts.
Monica smiles and says, "It's cute."
Chandler looks at her silently.
Chandler says, "And we're changing," turning back into his apartment.
The setting changes to a city street. Monica and Chandler jog side-by-side along the sidewalk. A song with vocals plays.
The song vocals sing, "My show," as they continue jogging.
Monica and Chandler jog past a parked yellow taxi.
The song vocals sing, "My"
Monica jogs ahead and looks back, while Chandler runs heavily behind her.
The song vocals continue, "joke. I got to be a macho." Monica points her arm forward as Chandler breathes heavily.
The setting changes back to the apartment. Chandler lies on his back on a rug while Monica kneels and holds his knees.
Monica says, "Come on, give me five more."
Chandler, breathing heavily, says, "Five more."
Chandler lets his head and shoulders drop back onto the rug.
Monica leans in slightly and says, "Five more and I'll flash you."
Chandler immediately sits up fast.
Chandler says, "Whoa,"
Chandler performs rapid sit-ups, crossing his arms over his chest, while Monica holds his knees.
During a sit-up, Chandler says, "Julie hat."
Chandler collapses flat onto his back, his arms resting on the floor.
Lying still, Chandler says, "Okay, just show me one of them."
Monica shakes her head and smiles. The setting changes to a coffee shop; a window sign reads "CENTRAL PERK".
Sitting on a sofa next to Joey, Chandler lifts a red mug and says, "Ow. Ow. Ow."
Chandler continues bringing the mug closer to his mouth, saying, "Ow. Ow. Ow."
Chandler takes a sip from the mug, narrowing his eyes as he says, "Ow. Ow. Ow."
Chandler slowly lowers the mug to the table, his eyes narrowed. Joey watches him.
Joey pushes the mug toward Chandler and says, "Ow. Out."
Chandler stares silently at Joey.
Turning his head slightly, Chandler says, "She's insane."
Chandler emphasizes, "The woman is insane."
Gesturing sharply with his hand, Chandler says, "It's before work. It's after work. It's during work. She's got me doing butt clenches at my desk"
Joey listens as Chandler drops his hand.
Chandler concludes, "and now they won't bring me my mail anymore."
Joey nods once.
Joey says, "Just tell her you want to stop." Chandler replies, "I can't, okay? While she's out of work, I'm all she's got. So that's"
Chandler gestures with an open palm and says, "why I was thinking maybe we could all pitch in and buy her her own restaurant."
Phoebe walks into the coffee shop and approaches the sofa.
Joey looks up and says, "Hey, babe."

Rachel walks past holding a tray and asks, "How'd it go with Scott last night?" Phoebe takes off her coat and says, "Oh, it was nice. Took him to a romantic restaurant, ordered champagne." Chandler adds, "Nice."
Chandler looks at Phoebe and says, "The guy still won't put," and Phoebe shakes her head, completing the sentence with, "nothing."
Chandler squints and tilts his head as Phoebe looks down.
Inside a coffee shop, Phoebe stands near the counter holding a dark garment. She touches her forehead with one hand.
Phoebe lowers her hand and says, "Look, I don't mind taking it slow."
"I like him a lot," Phoebe continues.
Phoebe turns her head slightly.
"He's really interesting, and he's really sweet," Phoebe says.
"And why won't he give it up?" Phoebe adds, gesturing with one hand.
Chandler and Joey sit on a nearby couch. Joey gestures toward Chandler.
Joey says, "Well, maybe he drives his car on the other side of the road, if you know what I mean."
Phoebe shakes her head.
"No. What do you mean? He's not British," Phoebe replies.
Joey and Chandler look at each other, Joey tilting his head.
Joey looks back at Phoebe and says, "Maybe he's gay."
"Oh," Phoebe says, looking upward.
Phoebe shakes her head.
Phoebe says, "no, I don't think that's the problem. Because we went dancing the other night and"
Phoebe clasps her hands together near her chest and continues, "just the way he held me so close and the way"
"he was looking into my eyes, I just definitely felt something," Phoebe says, keeping her hands clasped.
Rachel, sitting in a green armchair, says, "Yeah, but how much can you tell from a look?" overlapping with Phoebe who interjects, pointing to her hip, "No, I felt it on my hip. I could tell."
Monica walks over holding a tennis racket and a towel. Joey looks down at a newspaper while Chandler sits with his arms crossed.
Monica looks at Chandler and says, "Yo, Bing."
"Racquetball in 20 minutes," Monica adds.
Joey turns his head toward Chandler.
Chandler, keeping his arms crossed, says, "Joey, be a pal. Lift up my hand and smack her with it."
Through the coffee shop window, Ross and Julie are visible outside, kissing.
Phoebe points toward the window and says, "Rachel, don't look."
"What?" Rachel asks, turning her head toward the window.
Rachel looks out the window, then turns back to face the group.
Rachel wipes a cup with a cloth and says, "Come on, you guys. I don't care."
"I have a date tonight," Rachel adds.
Joey looks at her and asks, "Whoa, you have a date?"
Rachel says, "Yeah, monica's setting me up."
Joey asks, "What about Ross?"
Rachel gestures with one hand and says, "Oh, my whole insane jealousy thing?"
Rachel wipes the cup and says, "Well, you know, as much fun as that was, I've decided to opt for sanity."
Joey leans forward slightly and asks, "So you're really okay about all this?"
Rachel places the cloth down and begins to stand up.
Rachel picks up a small tray with cups and says, "Oh, yeah. Come on. I'm moving on."
Walking past the couch, Rachel adds, "He can press her up against that window as much as he wants."
Rachel continues walking toward the door and says, "Ralak here. Throw her through the damn thing."
Ross and Julie enter the coffee shop.
"Hi, guys," Ross says as they walk in.
Ross and Julie step toward the couch area.
Ross looks at Monica and says, "Oh, Monica. I figured I'd come by tomorrow morning and pick up Fluffy's old cat toys."
Monica replies, "Okay."
Monica adds, "Only if you say his full name."
Ross says, "Can I come over tomorrow and pick up Fluffy Meowington's cat toy?"
Joey leans forward on the couch.
Joey asks, "It's all right. You're getting a cat."
Ross puts his arm around Julie and replies, "Actually, we're getting a cat."
Rachel stands nearby, holding the tray.
Rachel asks, "Together? Both of you? Together?"
Monica stands next to Rachel.
Julie smiles and says, "Yeah, we figure it'll live with Ross half the time and with me half the time."
Rachel replies, "Oh, well, isn't that just lovely?"
Rachel smiles, keeping her lips pressed together.
Rachel says, "That's something the two of you will be able to enjoy for a really long time." Ross adds, "Hopefully."
Rachel looks at her bare wrist and says, "Wow, look at that. I got to go. I got a date with a man."
Rachel begins to walk away, and Ross says, "Okay."
Rachel walks to the coat rack near the door.
Rachel pulls her jacket from the rack and says, "You guys have a really good night."
Julie replies, "And you, too."
Rachel turns back around.
"Have a really good cat," Rachel says.
Rachel turns to exit, but stops abruptly, turning back around to walk to a small table near the door.
"Okay," Rachel says.
Placing the tray and cups on the table, Rachel adds, "We're not supposed to take these when we leave."
The setting changes to the exterior of a building at night. A neon sign reading "The Saloon" is visible.
Now inside the restaurant, Rachel sits at a table across from Michael. A waiter passes by in the background.
Michael gestures with his hands and says, "I don't know if Monica told you, but this is the first date I've gone on since my divorce,"
Lowering his hands, Michael adds, "so I seem a little nervous."
Rachel listens, resting her chin on her clasped hands.
"I am," Michael says.
Rachel keeps her gaze on Michael.
"How long do cats live?" Rachel asks.
Michael furrows his brow slightly.
Michael asks, "I'm sorry?"
Rachel repeats, "Cats."
"How long do they live?" Rachel asks.
"Figuring you don't, you know, throw them under a bus or something?" Rachel continues.
Michael tilts his head slightly.
"Maybe 15, 16 years," Michael replies.
Rachel picks up her wine glass and says, "That's just great."
Michael picks up his glass.
"Cheers," Rachel says.
They extend their glasses toward each other.
"Right?" Michael says.
A "Clink" sound is heard as their glasses touch.
Rachel smiles with her lips pressed tightly together.
In a restaurant, Rachel and Michael are seated across from each other at a table with a small lamp. Rachel's lips are pressed together and her eyes are lowered, while Michael looks at her.
Rachel briefly closes her eyes and exhales slightly. Michael keeps his gaze on her.
"Monica told you I was cuter than this, didn't she?" Michael asks. Rachel raises her eyebrows and shakes her head.
"Oh, no, Michael. It's not," Rachel replies, continuing to shake her head.
Rachel's gaze drops slightly as she says, "...you. Sorry."
Rachel continues to look downward, her mouth closed.
Rachel pinches the bridge of her nose, then lowers her hand. "It's this thing. It's probably not as bad as it sounds," she says.
Rachel gestures with both hands. "But this friend of mine is getting a cat with his," she continues.
"...girlfriend," Rachel adds. Michael nods slowly, saying, "Oh, that does sound..."
Michael trails off, maintaining a neutral expression. Rachel presses her lips together.
Rachel raises her hands with palms open, moving them outward. "I mean, he just started going out with her," she says.
Michael leans back slightly in his chair. "Is this guy an old boyfriend?" he asks.
Rachel laughs, closing her eyes and bringing one hand up near her face.
Lowering her hand, Rachel smiles and shakes her head. "Yeah, he wishes," she says.
Michael nods slowly, pressing his lips together and looking down briefly before looking back at her.
Rachel's smile fades as she looks down. "Oh, I'm sorry. Look at me," she says.
Rachel lifts her hands, gesturing toward Michael.
"Okay, Michael, let's talk about all right. Okay," Rachel says. Michael nods.
Rachel clasps her hands together under her chin, resting her elbows on the table and smiling.
"So," Rachel says.
Rachel maintains her position with her hands clasped under her chin. Michael waits, keeping his gaze on her.
"You ever get a pet with a girlfriend?" Rachel asks. Michael's eyebrows rise slightly.
Michael's lips press together in a brief smile, and he shakes his head slightly. The setting changes to a coffee shop.
Phoebe walks to a seating area carrying two mugs and places them on a low table. "So I figured it out," she says. Joey sits on a couch reading a magazine.
"What?" Joey asks. Phoebe sits next to him. "Why Scott doesn't want to sleep with me. It's because I'm not sexy enough," she says.
Joey lowers his magazine, looking toward Phoebe.
"Phoebe, that's crazy," Joey says. Phoebe takes a sip from her mug. Joey continues, "When I first met you, you know what I said to Chandler?"
Joey gestures with one hand. "I said excellent butt. Great rack," he says.
Phoebe looks at Joey with her lips slightly parted, then a smile forms on her face. Joey nods slightly.
Phoebe's smile widens. "Really? So sweet," she says.
Joey smiles and points a finger at her.
Phoebe's smile fades. "I mean, I'm officially offended," she says.
Joey leans toward her, gesturing with his hands. "Sweet peeps. Look, if you want to know what the deal is," he says.
"You're just going to have to ask them," Joey concludes.
"You're right," Phoebe replies, nodding.
Phoebe continues to look at Joey.
"You're right. You are so yum," Phoebe says, leaning in and wrapping her arms around Joey.
Joey hugs her back, patting her shoulder, both smiling. Through the window behind them, Ross walks past outside, accompanied by a woman.
Chandler runs up behind Ross and the woman outside the window, pointing and moving his hands while speaking. The woman turns to face Chandler.
Chandler holds his hands up with his palms facing outward. The woman slaps Chandler across the face.
Chandler recoils, rubbing his cheek. Ross looks at the woman and gestures with his hands. The setting changes back to the restaurant.
Rachel pours white wine from a bottle into her glass. A waitress stands beside the table holding a plate.
Rachel places the wine bottle down. The waitress sets the plate in front of Michael. Rachel gestures with one hand while speaking.
"Bugs. You know?" Rachel says, gesturing. "One of those fruit flies those things that live for, like, a day or something."
Rachel snaps her fingers repeatedly, leaning forward. "What are they called? What are they called?" she asks.
Michael raises his hands slightly, palms open.
"Fruit flies," Michael says.
Rachel points at him. "Yes," she says.
The waitress steps closer to the table.
"Thank you," Rachel says. The waitress looks at them and asks, "So would you like any desserts?"
"No, no desserts," Michael replies, shaking his head.
"Just check, please," Michael adds, looking up at the waitress.
The waitress turns and walks away. Rachel rests her hand over her mouth, looking at Michael.
Rachel lowers her hand. "You're not having fun, are you?" she asks. Michael shakes his head slightly and says, "No, I am."
"But only because for the last hour and a half I've been playing the movie Diner in my head," Michael continues.
Rachel's eyes widen, her mouth slightly open. Michael looks at her with his lips pressed firmly together.
In a dimly lit restaurant, Rachel sits at a table across from Michael, resting her head in her hands.
Rachel drops her hands and gestures outward. "Look at me. Look at me. I'm on a date with really great guy. All I can" she says.
"think about is Ross and his cat and his Julie," Rachel continues, gesturing toward Michael, who looks at her while eating.
Michael looks at Rachel as she finishes speaking.
Michael gestures slightly and asks, "Look, do you want a piece of pie, or"
Rachel throws her hands up and leans back in her chair.
Rachel runs a hand through her hair and says, "I'm too late for that, man. I've just, I've screwed this all up."
"I just want to get over him. God, I just want why can't I do" Rachel continues, leaning forward.
"that?" Rachel finishes.
Michael looks at Rachel, moving his hands as he prepares to speak.
"Look, I've been through a divorce," Michael says.
"Trust me, you're going to be fine," Michael continues, gesturing with his hands.
"You just can't see it now because you haven't had any closures. No," Michael says, shaking his head slightly.
Rachel's expression shifts as she looks at Michael.
"Closure," Rachel says, pointing a finger at him.
Rachel leans forward, keeping her finger pointed.
"That's what it is. That's what I need," Rachel says, her volume rising.
"God, you're brilliant," Rachel says, pressing both hands against her face.
Michael looks down slightly.
Rachel rubs her forehead and asks, "Why didn't I think of that? How do I get that?"
Michael gestures with one hand.
"Well, there's no one way, really," Michael says.
"It's just whatever it takes" Michael continues, gesturing.
"so that you can finally say to him I'm over you," Michael adds, pointing a finger.
Rachel stares ahead.
"Over you," Rachel says, her gaze directed away from Michael.
"That's what it is," Rachel continues.
Rachel shifts her posture, turning her head back toward Michael.
"Closure," Rachel says.
Rachel settles back in her chair. A man is visible sitting at a table behind her.
The man at the next table holds a cell phone to his ear and says, "Hello." Rachel turns around and says, "Excuse me."
The man continues talking on his phone.
Rachel stands up slightly, leans toward the man's table, and says, "Excuse me."
The man stops eating and looks at Rachel.
"Hang on," the man says into his phone, then turns to Rachel and says, "Hello?"
"What? Hi. I'm sorry. I need to borrow your phone for just 1 minute," Rachel says to the man.
The man stares at Rachel.
"I'm talking," the man says, holding the phone.
"I can see that. It's one phone call. I'll be very quick," Rachel responds.
"I'll even pay for it myself," Rachel adds, gesturing with her hand.
The man looks at her, keeping the phone near his ear.
"Okay, you're being a little weird about your phone," Rachel says.
The man sighs, looks at his phone, and raises it back to his ear.
"I'll call you back," the man says into the phone.
The man lowers the phone and extends his arm, holding the phone out to Rachel.
"Thank you," Rachel says, taking the phone from his hand.
Rachel sits back down at her table, holding the phone.
"So good with people," Rachel murmurs, looking at the phone.
Rachel presses buttons on the cell phone while Michael watches her.
Rachel lifts the phone to her ear and closes her eyes.
"Machine," Rachel says softly.
Michael clasps his hands together on the table.
"just waiting for the beat," Rachel says, keeping the phone to her ear.
"Good," Michael replies softly.
Rachel keeps the phone to her ear, listening.
"Ross," Rachel says.
"Hi, it's Rachel," she continues into the phone.
Rachel pauses.
"I'm just calling to say that everything's fine," Rachel says, nodding slightly.
"And I'm really happy for you and your cat," Rachel continues.
Rachel shifts her gaze toward Michael.
"Who, by the way, I think you should name Michael," Rachel says, gesturing toward Michael with her free hand.
Michael raises his eyebrows, looking slightly surprised. Rachel turns her attention back to the call.
"And you see there, I'm thinking of names. So obviously, I am over you," Rachel says, using her hand to emphasize her words.
Rachel pauses briefly.
"I am over you," Rachel says firmly.
"And that, my friend, is what they call" Rachel continues, pointing her index finger.
"closure," Rachel says, nodding.
Rachel lowers the phone, turns, and tosses it backward over her shoulder. The phone lands inside an ice bucket on the man's table with an audible clatter.
The setting changes to an exterior view of the New York City skyline at sunrise, showing skyscrapers including the Twin Towers.
The sun sits low on the horizon, glowing brightly next to the skyline.
The view frames the skyline from underneath the dark structure of a bridge.
The setting changes to an apartment hallway. A woman with dark hair stands in front of a green door labeled with the number 19, knocking on it.
Chandler, wearing a plaid bathrobe, flings the door open, walks quickly past the woman into his kitchen, and waves his hands.
"Monica, sunday morning," Chandler says, continuing to walk into the kitchen.
Chandler stops, points a finger, and says, "I'm not running on a Sunday." Monica stands in the doorway and asks, "Why not?" Chandler replies, "Because it's Sunday," his volume elevated.
Monica looks at Chandler.
"It's God's day," Chandler states.
Monica smiles slightly as Chandler stands in the kitchen.
In the kitchen, Chandler, wearing a plaid robe over a t-shirt, stands facing Monica. Monica wears a white long-sleeve shirt with a sweater tied around her waist.
Monica points at Chandler and says, "You say stop, and we stop."
Chandler closes his eyes briefly and wrinkles his nose.
Chandler opens his mouth wide and says loudly, "Okay, stop." Audience laughter begins.

Monica steps quickly toward Chandler, shaking her head and gesturing with both hands. "No, come on. We can't stop. Come on. We got three more pounds to go," she says, her pace fast, speaking over the continuing laughter.
Monica points to her chest, then sweeps her arm outward toward Chandler, saying, "I am the energy train, and you are on board." The laughter subsides.
Monica pumps her fist up and down in a pulling motion and leans toward Chandler, vocalizing, "Woo, woo, woo, woo, woo woo," her pitch rising. Chandler stares at her as audience laughter resumes.
Monica holds her fist up, smiling with her mouth open. Chandler remains still, keeping his gaze on her. The setting darkens until nothing is visible, and the laughter fades out.
The visual remains completely dark, and the audio is silent.
